faq.title: Common Questions
faq.q1: What's included in the monthly retainer?
faq.a1: Every plan includes technical SEO monitoring, link building, content optimization, and monthly reporting with revenue attribution. The difference between plans is the volume and intensity of link building and content production.
faq.a2: Start with the Strategy Audit ($500) — it gives you a clear picture of your current situation and which plan makes the most sense for your goals. If you're just getting started with SEO, Base is usually right. If you're already getting traffic and want to scale, Growth or Hyper Growth.
faq.a3: 3 months minimum for monthly retainers. SEO is a compounding activity — the first month is all foundation. Month 2-3 is where you start seeing traction. Clients who stick with it 6+ months see the best results.
faq.a4: Technical fixes can have an impact in 2-4 weeks. Keyword rankings typically improve at 60-90 days. Revenue attribution usually becomes clear at 3-6 months. I'll set realistic expectations in your first audit.
faq.a5: The Strategy Audit is non-refundable once delivered — you're paying for the analysis and my time. Monthly retainers can be cancelled at the end of any month after the 3-month minimum.

finalCta.h2Part1: Not sure which plan is right?
finalCta.body: A $500 strategy audit will tell you exactly where you stand, what's holding you back, and which plan makes sense for your specific situation.

problem.h2Part1: Startups don't need more blog posts. They need an

10 blog posts per month that nobody reads

approach.subhead: I don't do 6-month audits. We start driving results in weeks, not quarters.

approach.phase2Desc: Prioritized 90-day roadmap. High-impact, low-effort wins first — because startups can't wait 6 months.

approach.phase4Desc: Weekly reports tied to your north star metric. What works gets scaled. What doesn't gets cut. No vanity metrics.

included.card2Desc: Revenue-mapped keyword strategy aligned to your ICP, buyer journey, and competitive landscape.
included.card3Desc: Blog posts, landing pages, and pillar content that ranks and converts — written for humans, optimized for Google.

included.card6Desc: Weekly activity reports + monthly deep dives tied to pipeline, leads, and revenue — not just traffic.

pricing.subhead: All plans include the same activities. The difference is in link building intensity and execution speed.

whyMe.card1Desc: At agencies, your strategy is built by a senior then handed to a junior. With me, the senior does the work.
whyMe.card2Desc: I understand both the US and LatAm markets natively. If your startup serves both, I'm your unfair advantage.
whyMe.card3Desc: Electrical engineering background. I treat SEO as a system — inputs, outputs, feedback loops. Data over opinions.

faq.q1: How long until I see results?
faq.a1: You'll see technical improvements and quick wins in weeks 2-4. Meaningful traffic and ranking improvements typically happen in months 2-3. Revenue impact depends on your sales cycle, but most clients see measurable pipeline contribution by month 4.
faq.a2: No. Month-to-month engagement. I earn the relationship through results, not contracts. Either party can cancel at any time.
faq.q3: What if I already have an in-house marketing person?
faq.a3: Great — I work alongside your team, not against them. I handle the technical SEO execution and strategy while your marketer focuses on brand, social, and other channels. I also train them so your internal capabilities grow.
faq.q4: Do you work with pre-revenue startups?
faq.a4: Yes, but I'm transparent: if you don't have product-market fit yet, SEO might not be your highest-leverage channel right now. I'll tell you honestly in the free audit whether SEO is the right investment at your stage.
faq.a5: Google Search Console, GA4, Ahrefs, Semrush, Screaming Frog, Surfer, and AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude) for content optimization. All data is shared with you transparently.
faq.q6: What makes your SEO different for startups vs enterprises?
faq.a6: Speed and focus. Enterprises can afford to wait 12 months and optimize 500 pages. Startups need to identify the 10 pages that matter most and make them rank fast. My approach prioritizes revenue impact per hour invested.

meta.title: Generative Engine Optimization Services | Alfredo Abreu — B2B SEO Consultant
meta.description: Get your brand cited by ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Copilot, and Google AI Overviews. Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) services engineered for B2B visibility in AI search engines.

hero.body: Google is no longer the only search engine that matters. ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, and Copilot are answering your customers' questions right now — and if your brand isn't in those answers, your competitors are. I engineer your content to be cited, referenced, and recommended by AI.

shift.h2Part1: Your customers are already using AI to find solutions.
shift.h2Highlight: Is your brand in those answers?
shift.body: Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) is the discipline of engineering your brand's content, authority, and structured data so that large language models cite you when answering user queries. It's not replacing traditional SEO — it's the next layer on top of it.

comparison.h2Part1: Traditional SEO gets you ranked. GEO gets you
leftItems: Goal: rank on page 1 of SERPs
Content needs to be crawled and indexed
rightItems: Goal: be cited in AI-generated answers
Success metric: citations and mentions across AI engines
Content needs to be machine-readable and authoritative
Competes for the single AI-generated answer
comparison.bottomNote: The best strategy combines both. I do.

method.h2Part1: A 4-pillar system to get your brand
method.phase1Desc: I engineer structured data that makes your content machine-readable and citation-ready for LLMs. Organization schema, FAQ schema, HowTo schema, and custom schemas tailored to your industry.
method.phase2Desc: I configure your robots.txt to allow and prioritize the right AI crawlers — GPTBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended, ClaudeBot — so they can access and cite your content.
method.phase3Desc: I create and deploy a dedicated LLMs.txt file that tells AI engines exactly what your site is, what it offers, what makes it authoritative, and how to cite it. Think of it as a cover letter for AI.
method.phase4Desc: I monitor your brand's mentions and citations across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Copilot, and Google AI Overviews using Ahrefs AI citation tracking. What gets cited gets doubled down. What doesn't gets re-engineered.

included.h2: Everything you need to dominate AI search.
included.card1Desc: Complete analysis of your current AI search visibility — where you're cited, where you're missing, and where your competitors appear instead.
included.card2Desc: Custom JSON-LD schemas designed to make your content the most machine-readable option in your industry.
included.card3Desc: Dedicated AI-facing files (LLMs.txt, optimized robots.txt) that guide AI engines to your most authoritative content.
included.card4Desc: Content engineered to be cited — not just ranked. Definitive guides, data-backed research, and expert-positioned articles.
included.card5Desc: Monthly tracking of your brand's citations across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Copilot, and Google AI Overviews via Ahrefs.
included.card6Desc: GEO doesn't replace SEO — it builds on top of it. Technical SEO, link building, and content optimization are included to ensure both Google and AI engines trust your site.

pricing.h2: Two ways to get started with GEO.
plan2Features: Weekly reports + monthly AI citation report

future.h2Part1: By 2027, AI search will handle 40% of all queries.
future.card1Desc: AI Overviews and AI-generated answers mean fewer users click through to websites. If you're not in the AI answer, you don't exist.
future.card2Desc: LLMs cite sources they perceive as authoritative, well-structured, and trustworthy. Engineering that perception is what GEO does.
future.card3Desc: Most of your competitors haven't heard of GEO yet. The window to establish your brand as the AI-cited authority in your niche is now.

faq.a1: GEO is the practice of optimizing your brand's content and technical setup so that AI search engines — ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Copilot, and Google AI Overviews — cite and recommend you when answering user queries. It's the AI-era evolution of SEO.
faq.q2: Do I still need traditional SEO if I do GEO?
faq.a2: Yes. GEO builds on top of traditional SEO — it doesn't replace it. AI engines heavily rely on Google rankings, domain authority, and backlink profiles to determine which sources to cite. A strong SEO foundation makes GEO significantly more effective.
faq.q3: How do you track AI citations?
faq.a3: I use Ahrefs' AI citation tracking to monitor your brand's mentions across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Copilot, and Google AI Overviews. You get a monthly report showing citation count, which pages are being cited, and how your visibility compares to competitors.
faq.a4: LLMs.txt is a dedicated text file placed on your website (similar to robots.txt) that tells AI engines what your site is about, what your key offerings are, and how to properly cite you. It's like a structured cover letter specifically for AI crawlers.
faq.q5: How long until I see AI citations?
faq.a5: It depends on your existing domain authority and content quality. Sites with strong SEO foundations can start appearing in AI answers within 4-8 weeks of GEO implementation. New or low-authority sites may take 3-6 months as we build the traditional SEO foundation simultaneously.
faq.q6: Can you guarantee my brand will be cited by ChatGPT?
faq.a6: No — and anyone who guarantees AI citations is not being honest. AI engines are non-deterministic and change constantly. What I can guarantee is that your site will be technically optimized, content-engineered, and authority-positioned to maximize your probability of being cited. My track record of 770+ citations across 5 engines speaks for itself.

finalCta.h2: Ready to be cited by AI?
finalCta.body: Book a free 30-minute GEO audit. I'll check your current AI search visibility, show you where your competitors are being cited, and map out a plan to get your brand into AI answers.
